llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm exists (human_ai). This is compared against the human-only baseline (human_human) where participants collaborated with other humans.
llm_extracted.notes: The paper reports a 50% increase in productivity (ads per worker per unit time) for human-AI vs human-human teams. A regression coefficient β=1.954 (SE=0.197) is also reported for the same comparison. The outcome transformation (log, asinh, or levels) is not explicitly stated in the provided data, so regression_outcome_is_log is left null. The measurement unit is 'count of ad submissions' which is a throughput/productivity measure, so larger_is_better. Additional regression coefficients are reported for communication and delegation effects but these are not the primary human-AI vs human-human comparison.
source_quotes: Participants in human-AI teams submitted 50% more ads per worker, per unit time than their counterparts in human-human teams, with results consistent when demographic controls are added (Table 2).
